Uva Ursi (Arctostaphylos uva-ursi) is a low-growing, woody, evergreen shrub native to North America, commonly known as bearberry or mountain box. The plant is valued for its health benefits, which include treating urinary tract infections, reducing inflammation, and soothing digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Uva Ursi include arbutin, hydroquinone, and flavonoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Uva Ursi is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions.
